reforms.description: France has increased research tax credits to bolster R&D activities, aiming to enhance the economy's supply potential and stimulate innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased R&D tax credits incentivize private sector investment in research, which can lead to higher productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has taken actions to increase research tax credits.
reforms.importance_rationale: The increase in research tax credits represents a significant policy adjustment aimed at enhancing R&D investment, which is crucial for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Measures include increased research tax credits to bolster R&D activities.

reforms.description: To enhance the effectiveness of the competitiveness clusters policy, it is recommended that state aid be contingent on results, with a sunset date for subsidies and a gradual shift towards private financing.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Linking state aid to results encourages efficiency and effectiveness in innovation funding, potentially leading to better outcomes in competitiveness and economic growth.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in how public funding is allocated, aiming to improve the effectiveness of existing policies without a complete overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: To make the 'competitiveness clusters' policy more effective, maintenance of state aid should be contingent on results; establish a sunset date for subsidies while gradually replacing them with private financing.

reforms.description: The Garantie des Risques Locatifs (Tenancy Guarantee Scheme) was established to facilitate access to private-sector housing for low-income households by insuring landlords against tenant default risks. The scheme was reformed in January 2010 to expand eligibility to all tenants with an effort ratio of 50% or lower, regardless of their profile.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing the risk for landlords, the scheme encourages them to rent to low-income households, thereby increasing housing supply and mobility in the market.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme was reformed and extended in January 2010.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform significantly enhances access to housing for low-income households, addressing a critical barrier in the housing market.
reforms.source_quote: The Garantie des Risques Locatifs (Tenancy Guarantee Scheme –GRL), which was established under the impetus of the social partners, is aimed at making it easier for low-income households to access private-sector housing stock in areas of excess demand by insuring landlords against the risk of tenant default.

reforms.description: The French government has implemented measures to enhance the autonomy of universities, allowing them greater flexibility in managing their resources and academic programs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased autonomy for universities is expected to improve their efficiency and responsiveness to labor market needs, thereby enhancing the overall supply of skilled labor.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has taken steps to grant universities more autonomy.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in the governance of higher education, aiming to enhance the effectiveness of universities in contributing to innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Greater autonomy for universities is part of the government's strategy to boost the supply potential of the economy.

reforms.description: The French government has introduced a research tax credit aimed at incentivizing private sector investment in R&D activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing tax incentives for R&D, the reform encourages firms to invest more in innovation, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The research tax credit has been established to stimulate private sector R&D investment.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is a major policy tool aimed at enhancing private sector R&D investment, which is crucial for fostering innovation and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The research tax credit is designed to boost private investment in R&D activities.

reforms.description: The French government increased the research tax credit to enhance R&D investment, although it requires further review to prevent potential abuses.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing R&D investment, the reform aims to boost innovation and productivity in the private sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The research tax credit was increased as part of the government's measures.
reforms.importance_rationale: The increase in the research tax credit is a significant measure aimed at enhancing R&D investment, which is crucial for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The government is increasing the research tax credit, although this provision should be reviewed in order to correct possible abuses.

reforms.description: The French government is investing in higher education, training, and research to create top-ranked universities as part of its broader economic recovery strategy.
reforms.component_name: Investment in Higher Education and Research
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in higher education and research is expected to enhance the skill level of the workforce, leading to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is investing in higher education and research as part of its recovery plan.
reforms.importance_rationale: This investment is significant for enhancing the quality of education and research, which is vital for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The government is investing in higher education, training and research with a view to creating top-ranked universities.

reforms.description: The establishment of advanced R&D centres (Pôles de recherche et d’enseignement supérieur, PRES) initiated closer cooperation between grandes écoles and universities, with four created in 2009 and three in 2010, totaling 18, supported by 'centres of excellence' as part of 'investments for the future'.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhances collaboration between educational institutions and promotes research capabilities, which can lead to innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Four advanced R&D centres were created in 2009 and three in 2010.
reforms.importance_rationale: The creation of R&D centres represents a significant step towards enhancing research collaboration and infrastructure, which is crucial for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The establishment of advanced R&D centres (Pôles de recherche et d’enseignement supérieur, PRES), which initiated closer co-operation between the grandes écoles and the universities in 2007, is continuing.

reforms.description: The research tax credit, one of the most generous R&D support mechanisms globally, was evaluated in 2010, confirming its leverage effect on private expenditure and recommending its stability until at least 2013.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing substantial tax incentives for R&D, the credit encourages private sector investment in innovation, which can enhance productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The research tax credit was evaluated and recommended for stability until at least 2013.
reforms.importance_rationale: The evaluation and recommendation for stability of the research tax credit reflect its significant role in supporting R&D investment in the private sector.
reforms.source_quote: The research tax credit, with a tax expenditure of EUR4.5 billion in 2010 and 2.1 billion projected for 2011, is one of the most generous R&D support mechanisms in the world.

reforms.description: The French government committed to a EUR11 billion cut in tax expenditures for 2011 and annual cuts of EUR3 billion for 2012-14, aiming to improve fiscal sustainability and reduce inefficiencies in the tax system.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Reducing inefficient tax expenditures can increase government revenues and improve the allocation of resources, potentially enhancing overall economic efficiency.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has committed to specific cuts in tax expenditures for the years 2011-2014.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the approach to tax expenditures, aiming to enhance fiscal sustainability and efficiency in public finances.
reforms.source_quote: The government has committed to a EUR11 billion cut in tax expenditures for 2011 and annual cuts of EUR3 billion for 2012-14.

reforms.description: The French government has implemented feed-in tariffs to promote renewable energy, ensuring that electricity producers can at least break even on their investments. These tariffs are set above market prices for various renewable energy sources, including solar and wind, to encourage the development of these technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing financial incentives for renewable energy production, the feed-in tariffs encourage investment in clean technologies, which can lead to increased innovation and job creation in the sector.
reforms.status_evidence: Feed-in tariffs were first introduced in 2001-02 for electricity generation technologies.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of feed-in tariffs represents a significant structural change in France's energy policy, aimed at fostering the growth of renewable energy sources and reducing reliance on fossil fuels.
reforms.source_quote: Feed-in tariffs were first introduced in 2001-02 for electricity generation technologies that make use of solar, wind, tidal wave, geothermal and hydro energy.

reforms.description: The Grenelle laws mandate the obligatory display of the environmental impact of consumer products, aiming to cover all products across sectors, with a trial period starting in July 2011.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative is expected to enhance consumer awareness and drive demand for environmentally friendly products, potentially leading to innovation in sustainable product design.
reforms.status_evidence: A trial period will be launched in July 2011.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift towards transparency in environmental impacts, which can influence consumer behavior and promote innovation in product development.
reforms.source_quote: The Grenelle laws set the ambitious goal of requiring an obligatory displaying of the over-the-lifecycle environmental impact of consumer products.
